run.true_intent: I run customer support out of a Gmail inbox. I want it to read incoming support emails, summarise each one, label the urgent ones, and draft a reply for the top few — but I always want to review before anything is sent. Nothing auto-sends. I don't want it touching my personal email, just the support address.
run.vague_intent: make me a bot that helps with my emails

transcript.question: What kind of email companion do you want?
options: A: Daily briefing — scan my inbox once a day and surface what matters in a ranked digest
B: Real-time triage — watch for new messages and alert me the moment something urgent arrives
C: Interactive assistant — I ask it questions about my inbox on demand (search, summarize threads, draft replies)
transcript.answer: A daily briefing â€” one scan a day over the support inbox is enough for me.

transcript.question: What should "Thread Deep-Dive" remember between runs? (Pick "Nothing" if it doesn't need memory.)
options: Items I've already approved or rejected
Recurring context (people, projects, topics I care about)
transcript.answer: Recurring context â€” the people and topics that keep coming up, so it recognises repeat customers and ongoing issues.

transcript.question: Should "Daily Support Digest" wait for your approval before publishing its output?
options: Never — auto-publish; I can undo/discard myself
On low confidence — only pause when unsure
Always — I want to sign off every run
transcript.answer: Always â€” I want to sign off every run. Nothing goes out without me reading it first.

transcript.question: Which storage connector should "Daily Support Digest" use?
transcript.answer: Google Drive â€” the digest should land as a doc in the same Drive folder as our support notes.

transcript.question: How should "Daily Support Digest" fire?
options: A: On demand — I'll trigger it manually
C: When an external event occurs (e.g. new document, inbound message)
transcript.answer: B â€” on a schedule, once every weekday morning so I can go through the digest with my coffee.

capabilities.title: Daily Support Digest
capabilities.description: Every weekday morning, a ranked briefing is drafted for user sign-off, then written as a Google Doc into the support notes Drive folder.

subscriptions.description: Emitted only after user approves and the Drive doc is written successfully.

capabilities.title: Thread Deep-Dive
capabilities.description: On-demand full summary of a specific email thread — drafted and held for user sign-off before finalising.

trigger.description: On demand — user supplies a thread identifier or subject keyword and requests a summary.
